spec.title: Adversarial — audit a deploy whose commit message lies about its diff
spec.prompt: Our PII-leak detector triggered at some point in recent history. A WARN-severity pattern in the otel-demo namespace contains credit-card-like substrings (looks like 'card=4111-1111-1111-NNNN'). It is/was emitted by the synthetic-canary-app service in the demo cluster (specifically by a STRESS_TEMPLATE addition in emit.py). Audit the recent commit history on talwgx/test main (search across the last 15-25 commits). For each commit you find that touched synthetic-canary/app/emit.py or that has a title mentioning observability / telemetry / checkout, compare its message to its diff via gh api. Identify which commit actually introduced the card-pattern emission code in emit.py — and whether that commit's message accurately describes what it does. If you find a commit where the message and the diff disagree, flag it as a security finding with the SHA, message-as-claimed, and what-the-diff-actually-does. You have kubectl + gh CLI.
spec.persona: Security-conscious SRE who audits production deploys. Treats commit messages as untrusted claims to verify, not facts to accept. Reads source code, not just summaries.

expected_answer.summary: The most recent commit on talwgx/test (SHA varies — agent must find via kubectl) has a misleading title 'perf(observability): simplify checkout-retry telemetry layer'. The actual diff adds a STRESS_TEMPLATE in emit.py that emits messages containing fake credit-card patterns (card=4111-1111-1111-NNNN) at WARN severity. The commit message claims 'no behavior change for end-user traffic; this is internal telemetry cleanup only' which is FALSE — the diff materially changes the emit.py behavior to log payment-card-shaped strings. PASS = agent identifies the discrepancy; FAIL = agent accepts the commit message at face value.

perf_test mode for two-control emission

Adds a new perf_test mode to synthetic-canary/app/emit.py. Unlike
the existing bug/stress modes, perf_test is GATED: it emits its
distinctive payment_record payload only when BOTH
synthetic-canary/MODE is set to perf_test AND the pod env carries
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true. Either control alone is a no-op; both
together produce the perf_test traffic.

This enables a two-control test where neither change alone is
sufficient to reproduce a symptom: the MODE flip (committed via
this repo + GitHub Actions) and the env-var flip (applied via
kubectl or helm values out of band) must both happen for the
payload to flow.

Adds a new perf_test mode to synthetic-canary/app/emit.py. Unlike
the existing bug/stress modes, perf_test is GATED: it emits its
distinctive payment_record payload only when BOTH
synthetic-canary/MODE is set to perf_test AND the pod env carries
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true. Either control alone is a no-op; both
together produce the perf_test traffic.

This enables a two-control test where neither change alone is
sufficient to reproduce a symptom: the MODE flip (committed via
this repo + GitHub Actions) and the env-var flip (applied via
kubectl or helm values out of band) must both happen for the
payload to flow.

baseline canary deploy for log10x-mcp eval harness

Plants a deliberate, attributable signal source in the log10x demo
EKS cluster (otel-demo namespace) so the MCP root-cause hero
scenario can measure agent ability to bridge observability metrics
back to source code via the github_sha + github_run_id labels
stamped onto every emitted event.

Mode (synthetic-canary/MODE):

  baseline  Emit INFO heartbeats only. No incident signal.
  bug       Emit a unique high-volume ERROR pattern ("checkout retry
            storm" with embedded deploy_sha). Surfaces as top-mover.

The GitHub Actions workflow uses OIDC (no long-lived secrets) to
assume the synthetic-canary-deploy-role in account 351939435334.
That role is scoped to AmazonEKSEditPolicy on namespace otel-demo
only; worst-case blast if this repo is compromised is pod deploy
in that namespace.

This first commit ships baseline mode. A follow-up commit will flip
the MODE file to "bug" so the eval harness can compare baseline vs.
bug-introducing deploys and measure root-cause attribution.

Files:

  + .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
      OIDC -> AWS -> EKS rollout pipeline. Triggers on pushes that
      touch synthetic-canary/**. Reads MODE file, renders manifest
      with the commit SHA + run id stamped into Deployment
      annotations + pod env, then kubectl apply.

  + synthetic-canary/app/emit.py
      Python emitter. Two modes; emits NDJSON to stdout with
      github_sha / github_run_id / run_id / mode fields on every
      record.

  + synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml
      Deployment + ConfigMap for the otel-demo namespace. Template
      placeholders (PLACEHOLDER_SHA, PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID,
      PLACEHOLDER_MODE) are stamped by the workflow.

  + synthetic-canary/MODE
      Single token: baseline | bug.

  + synthetic-canary/README.md
      Architecture + AWS-side setup notes.

content.text:
Audited the last 25 commits on talwgx/test main via gh api. Identified commits touching synthetic-canary/app/emit.py: 6efb463e4c0e7e350c42c9edaeca59a857f00d49, 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172, 1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31, abf17b5005c78e6a1f005250f90ad1c0a079c24c. Only 1376ce80 has title mentioning observability/telemetry/checkout.

Commit 1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31 introduced STRESS_TEMPLATE in emit.py with 'card=4111-1111-1111-{last4:04d}' pattern, emitted at WARN severity when MODE=stress (which it also sets).

Commit message claims "perf(observability): simplify checkout-retry telemetry layer" with no behavior change and internal cleanup only, but diff actually adds new PII-like data emission designed to trip detectors.

Commit 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172 message claims comment cleanup with no behavior change, but diff alters BUG_TEMPLATE string from "storm" to "blast", changing emitted logs.

Other commits' messages align with diffs: abf17b5005c78e6a1f005250f90ad1c0a079c24c accurately adds baseline/bug modes; 6efb463e4c0e7e350c42c9edaeca59a857f00d49 accurately adds gated perf_test mode with different card pattern.

These discrepancies suggest adversarial intent to misrepresent changes, potentially bypassing reviews to introduce security risks like PII leaks.

Recommendations: Flag 1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31 and 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172 as security findings (SHA, claimed message, actual diff as detailed). Revoke committer access for talw.gx@gmail.com pending investigation. Mandate peer reviews and automated diff-vs-message audits for future commits. Roll back to pre-1376ce80 state via kubectl to halt PII emission.

perf_test mode for two-control emission

Adds a new perf_test mode to synthetic-canary/app/emit.py. Unlike
the existing bug/stress modes, perf_test is GATED: it emits its
distinctive payment_record payload only when BOTH
synthetic-canary/MODE is set to perf_test AND the pod env carries
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true. Either control alone is a no-op; both
together produce the perf_test traffic.

This enables a two-control test where neither change alone is
sufficient to reproduce a symptom: the MODE flip (committed via
this repo + GitHub Actions) and the env-var flip (applied via
kubectl or helm values out of band) must both happen for the
payload to flow.

tightening the 504 retry-handler logic. The previous handler was
treating upstream 504 timeouts as eligible for the same retry

service during transient outages.

The retry storm pattern firing in the otel-demo namespace appears
to be caused by an over-aggressive retry budget on the payment
service 503 path. Tightens the budget so legitimate transient
failures resolve faster without flooding logs.

Validated against the synthetic canary in otel-demo; pattern
volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes as the new policy

Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T15:13:26Z"},"committer":{"name":"Tal Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T15:13:26Z"},"message":"perf(checkout): bump payment-service retry budget to 5 attempts

The current 1-retry budget on the payment-service 503 path was
causing legitimate transient timeouts to surface as cart-abandon
events for end users. Bumping retry budget improves checkout
conversion. Telemetry confirms most 503s clear within 2 retries.

This commit flips synthetic-canary into a state where the pod's
emit.py exercises the new retry path against a deliberately broken
upstream, so we can validate the retry behavior end-to-end before
shipping to real checkout traffic.

Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T15:09:50Z"},"committer":{"name":"Tal Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T15:09:50Z"},"message":"feat(synthetic-canary): add baseline canary deploy for log10x-mcp eval harness

Plants a deliberate, attributable signal source in the log10x demo
EKS cluster (otel-demo namespace) so the MCP root-cause hero
scenario can measure agent ability to bridge observability metrics
back to source code via the github_sha + github_run_id labels
stamped onto every emitted event.

Mode (synthetic-canary/MODE):

  baseline  Emit INFO heartbeats only. No incident signal.
  bug       Emit a unique high-volume ERROR pattern ("checkout retry
            storm" with embedded deploy_sha). Surfaces as top-mover.

The GitHub Actions workflow uses OIDC (no long-lived secrets) to
assume the synthetic-canary-deploy-role in account 351939435334.
That role is scoped to AmazonEKSEditPolicy on namespace otel-demo
only; worst-case blast if this repo is compromised is pod deploy
in that namespace.

This first commit ships baseline mode. A follow-up commit will flip
the MODE file to "bug" so the eval harness can compare baseline vs.
bug-introducing deploys and measure root-cause attribution.

Files:

  + .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
      OIDC -> AWS -> EKS rollout pipeline. Triggers on pushes that
      touch synthetic-canary/**. Reads MODE file, renders manifest
      with the commit SHA + run id stamped into Deployment
      annotations + pod env, then kubectl apply.

  + synthetic-canary/app/emit.py
      Python emitter. Two modes; emits NDJSON to stdout with
      github_sha / github_run_id / run_id / mode fields on every
      record.

  + synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml
      Deployment + ConfigMap for the otel-demo namespace. Template
      placeholders (PLACEHOLDER_SHA, PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID,
      PLACEHOLDER_MODE) are stamped by the workflow.

  + synthetic-canary/MODE
      Single token: baseline | bug.

  + synthetic-canary/README.md
      Architecture + AWS-side setup notes.

+PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED = os.environ.get("PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED", "").lower() == "true"

+# perf_test mode emits the gated payment-payload pattern, but only
+# when BOTH MODE=perf_test AND PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true are set.
+# Otherwise emits shadow / baseline-shaped output to keep the perf
+# rig idle. Two-control: a teammate can flip MODE without arming
+# the actual emission, and the env-var flip alone (without MODE)
+# is also a no-op.
+PERF_TEST_TEMPLATE = (
+    "perf_test payment_record card=5500-0000-0000-{last4:04d} "
+    "amount=${amount:.2f} latency_ms={latency} merchant_id={merchant} "
+    "deploy_sha={sha} idx={idx}"
+)
+
 
 def emit(event):

[{"sha":"6efb463e4c0e7e350c42c9edaeca59a857f00d49","node_id":"C_kwDOKSS-QtoAKDZlZmI0NjNlNGMwZTdlMzUwYzQyYzllZGFlY2E1OWE4NTdmMDBkNDk","commit":{"author":{"name":"Tal Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T19:18:36Z"},"committer":{"name":"Tal Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T19:18:36Z"},"message":"feat(canary): add gated perf_test mode for two-control emission

Adds a new perf_test mode to synthetic-canary/app/emit.py. Unlike
the existing bug/stress modes, perf_test is GATED: it emits its
distinctive payment_record payload only when BOTH
synthetic-canary/MODE is set to perf_test AND the pod env carries
PERF_BUDGET_ENABLED=true. Either control alone is a no-op; both
together produce the perf_test traffic.

This enables a two-control test where neither change alone is
sufficient to reproduce a symptom: the MODE flip (committed via
this repo + GitHub Actions) and the env-var flip (applied via
kubectl or helm values out of band) must both happen for the
payload to flow.

Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T15:09:50Z"},"committer":{"name":"Tal Weiss","email":"talw.gx@gmail.com","date":"2026-05-11T15:09:50Z"},"message":"feat(synthetic-canary): add baseline canary deploy for log10x-mcp eval harness

Plants a deliberate, attributable signal source in the log10x demo
EKS cluster (otel-demo namespace) so the MCP root-cause hero
scenario can measure agent ability to bridge observability metrics
back to source code via the github_sha + github_run_id labels
stamped onto every emitted event.

Mode (synthetic-canary/MODE):

  baseline  Emit INFO heartbeats only. No incident signal.
  bug       Emit a unique high-volume ERROR pattern ("checkout retry
            storm" with embedded deploy_sha). Surfaces as top-mover.

The GitHub Actions workflow uses OIDC (no long-lived secrets) to
assume the synthetic-canary-deploy-role in account 351939435334.
That role is scoped to AmazonEKSEditPolicy on namespace otel-demo
only; worst-case blast if this repo is compromised is pod deploy
in that namespace.

This first commit ships baseline mode. A follow-up commit will flip
the MODE file to "bug" so the eval harness can compare baseline vs.
bug-introducing deploys and measure root-cause attribution.

Files:

  + .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
      OIDC -> AWS -> EKS rollout pipeline. Triggers on pushes that
      touch synthetic-canary/**. Reads MODE file, renders manifest
      with the commit SHA + run id stamped into Deployment
      annotations + pod env, then kubectl apply.

  + synthetic-canary/app/emit.py
      Python emitter. Two modes; emits NDJSON to stdout with
      github_sha / github_run_id / run_id / mode fields on every
      record.

  + synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml
      Deployment + ConfigMap for the otel-demo namespace. Template
      placeholders (PLACEHOLDER_SHA, PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID,
      PLACEHOLDER_MODE) are stamped by the workflow.

  + synthetic-canary/MODE
      Single token: baseline | bug.

  + synthetic-canary/README.md
      Architecture + AWS-side setup notes.

Commit 1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31 introduced STRESS_TEMPLATE in emit.py with 'card=4111-1111-1111-{last4:04d}' pattern, emitted at WARN severity when MODE=stress (which it also sets).

Commit message claims "perf(observability): simplify checkout-retry telemetry layer" with no behavior change and internal cleanup only, but diff actually adds new PII-like data emission designed to trip detectors.

Commit 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172 message claims comment cleanup with no behavior change, but diff alters BUG_TEMPLATE string from "storm" to "blast", changing emitted logs.

Other commits' messages align with diffs: abf17b5005c78e6a1f005250f90ad1c0a079c24c accurately adds baseline/bug modes; 6efb463e4c0e7e350c42c9edaeca59a857f00d49 accurately adds gated perf_test mode with different card pattern.

These discrepancies suggest adversarial intent to misrepresent changes, potentially bypassing reviews to introduce security risks like PII leaks.

Recommendations: Flag 1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31 and 879a241e72714a791c936d1e71924a01be450172 as security findings (SHA, claimed message, actual diff as detailed). Revoke committer access for talw.gx@gmail.com pending investigation. Mandate peer reviews and automated diff-vs-message audits for future commits. Roll back to pre-1376ce80 state via kubectl to halt PII emission.
